verdicts.notes:
Adjudicated against verified ground truth: js/data/poses.js holds exactly the frozen 29-id library (arm-sweeps, baby-cobra, bird-dog, box-breath, bridge, butterfly, cat-cow, chair-pose, childs-pose, clamshell, down-dog, figure-four, forward-fold, goddess, happy-baby, hip-circles, kickbacks, kind-close, legs-up, low-lunge, neck-rolls, pelvic-breath, seated-twist, shoulder-rolls, side-reach, squats, thread-needle, tree-pose, warrior2); all 9 session additions are absent there, exactly matching their [PROPOSED NEW] tags, and each carries one of the three valid dispositions (reuse mapping, new rig, or documented caption-only fallback). js/state.js is at version 1 with a migrate() seam and no PAR-Q/chair-mode code, confirming screening enforcement, store v2, and chair/floor mode are genuine DoD build items, NOT per-session defects. The session correctly DECLARES the required gates and a named accessibility path, which is all the design gate requires.

Clinical design verdict — PASS as a design with minor notes. (1) cumulativeLoadOK: estTotalSecs 2706 ~= 45.1 min; all rest 25-30s; strength reps/sets sane (bridge 10-12x2, clamshell 12-15x2, squats 10-12x2, deadbug 6-8/side); only breath items are pelvic-breath ~50s, extended-exhale-breath 75-90s, kind-close affirmation 75-90s — all <= 90s. (2) pelvicFloorDiastasisSafe: the three cardio bouts (marching, step-touch, knee-drives) are DISTRIBUTED across the main block, not stacked, each buffered by strength/hold/mobility; bird-dog (primary anti-rotation core) sits mid-block while control is fresh and heel-slides primes core in warmup; exhale-on-exertion cued on every loaded move; global doming/coning stop-rule present in disclaimers + screening. This is Slightly Sweaty (not Super Sweaty) so the extended-after-SuperSweaty wind-down rule is N/A; a full ~20% exhale-led down-regulation wind-down precedes the close. (3) tierCeilingCompliant: no crunch/sit-up; bird-dog is quadruped anti-rotation; wall-incline-press explicitly INCLINE not floor plank; deadbug explicitly feet-supported, never double-leg lower; all cardio explicitly low-impact, feet grounded, no jump/impact. (4) screeningGatesCorrect: declares the standard postpartum clearance/consult line for this gentler tier and correctly states the Super-Sweaty gate (12+ wks, provider-cleared, symptom-free) is not required and that no screened-only moves are included. (5) accessibilityComplete: declares reduced-motion + aria-live caption fallback AND a named chair/floor substitution path (seated march/step-touch/knee-drives/squats/warrior2/wall-press; floor moves on bed/chair). Residual items are minor refinements only, not ship blockers for a design gate.
requiredFixes: Minor sequencing refinement: deadbug-supported (loaded anti-extension deep-core) sits at main position 9 immediately followed by knee-drives (single-leg dynamic trunk cardio) at position 10 — placing the most demanding core-control move plus late single-leg dynamic trunk work when the abdominal wall/pelvic floor is more fatigued. Consider moving knee-drives earlier (e.g. swap with step-touch) or inserting an explicit recovery beat between deadbug-supported and knee-drives so dynamic single-leg trunk work is not the last loaded core demand. Knee-drives is feet-planted/controlled (40s/side, no hop), which mitigates but does not eliminate the ordering concern.

Minor cue completeness: the doming/coning + pelvic-floor-pressure/leaking stop-rule is carried at the global disclaimer/screening level but is not echoed in the per-move tierNote of each loaded core/cardio item (bridge, bird-dog, deadbug-supported, marching, step-touch, knee-drives, wall-incline-press, squats). Add an explicit per-move 'stop if you see doming/coning or feel pelvic-floor pressure/leaking' check cue on each loaded core and cardio move for redundancy at the point of effort.

verdicts.notes:
Re-reviewed against the COMPLETE untruncated JSON and the actual codebase. Verdict: clinically sound AS A DESIGN; pass=true with minor notes.

CLINICAL DESIGN QUALITY (the five criteria I was asked to judge):
1) cumulativeLoadOK = true. estTotalSecs 1230 = 20:30, a sane fit for a No Time 20-min target. Both breath items are <=90s (box-breath arrival 90s, extended-exhale-breath close 90s — at the boundary, acceptable). Strength reps/sets are sane and explicitly compressed for the budget (squats 12-15x2 w/ 2s bottom pause + 20s rest; bird-dog 10/side x2 w/ 2-3s hold + 30s rest). Cardio is interval-dosed (knee-drives 45s/side, shadow-box 45s, step-out 55s) with stated rests. Doses + rests are plausible for super_sweaty in 20 min.
2) pelvicFloorDiastasisSafe = true. Arrival box-breath explicitly pre-connects deep core/pelvic floor before any load. Exhale-on-exertion is cued on the loaded moves (squats: exhale + gently lift pelvic floor on the way up; knee-drives: exhale on the drive, no breath-holding; shadow-box: short exhale per punch, never brace silently). Doming/coning stop-rule is cued on bird-dog ("watch for doming; regress to knee-down if midline bulges") and reinforced in disclaimers. Wind-down (low-lunge 40s/side + reclined-twist 50s/side + legs-up 75s) plus close (extended-exhale 90s + kind-close 75s) is a deliberately longer, parasympathetic-led down-regulation after Super Sweaty — appropriate and non-negotiable per intent. No banned moves (no crunch/sit-up/floor plank). bird-dog is the diastasis-safe anti-extension/anti-rotation core driver. See minor residual on main-block ordering below.
3) tierCeilingCompliant = true. All cardio is explicitly low-impact, no jumping, no impact landings (step-out side steps, knee-drives one-foot-grounded, shadow-box low-impact footwork). No flexion core. Super Sweaty intensity is achieved via tempo/density/supersets, not impact — genuinely within the postpartum ceiling.
4) screeningGatesCorrect = true. The session DECLARES the correct Super Sweaty gate (>=12 weeks postpartum AND provider/pelvic-PT clearance for vigorous/abdominal/impact), a pelvic-health symptom screen (leakage, heaviness/bulge, doming, pain, bleeding), an unmanaged-condition exclusion (diastasis/prolapse), and a correct fail-path (drop to Slightly Sweaty / No Sweat of same length, which carry the standard disclaimer not the clearance gate). Declaration is the bar here; app-side enforcement is a DoD item per framing.
5) accessibilityComplete = true. DECLARES reduced-motion (avatar honors setting, static-caption fallback, every cue mirrored in aria-live, every move skippable) AND a named chair/floor path with specific substitutions for the standing-cardio moves (knee-drives/squats-as-sit-to-stand/shadow-box seated in a sturdy chair; bird-dog as seated opposite-arm/opposite-leg reach; low-lunge as seated figure-four; legs-up on a chair seat). Self-pacing/march-down option stated. step-out is the only standing-cardio move whose seated substitution is not named verbatim — captured as a minor residual fix, not a blocker.

Bottom line: the design is safe and well-reasoned. Residual items are a real (minor) sequencing concern and two small declaration/copy completeness gaps, not safety failures.
requiredFixes: MINOR sequencing (prior concern #9, partially REAL): in the main block bird-dog (loaded deep-core driver) sits between two cardio intervals (knee-drives -> bird-dog -> shadow-box) with only 20-30s rest, so the diastasis-safe core hold is performed under accumulating cardio fatigue, the window when control/IAP management degrades and doming risk rises. Prefer placing bird-dog earlier (e.g., squats -> bird-dog -> knee-drives -> shadow-box) so core work happens while control is fresh, OR extend the recovery beat before bird-dog beyond 20s. Doming stop-rule and knee-down regression already cued, so this is a refinement not a safety failure.

MINOR accessibility completeness (prior concern #8, partially REAL): the chair/floor path names seated substitutions for knee-drives, squats, shadow-box, bird-dog, low-lunge and legs-up, but does NOT name a seated alternative for step-out-warmup. Add an explicit seated/marching substitution for step-out-warmup so every standing-cardio move has a declared accessibility path.

MINOR copy/data discipline (prior concern #7, partially REAL): the four PROPOSED NEW moves and extended-exhale-breath are legitimately not yet in exercises.js (confirmed), and box-breath is frozen at secs=75/blocks:[close] while this session doses it at 90s in the arrive phase. As a DoD note: when the new ids are appended, drive arrival/per-move timing from the data records (and add 'arrive'/'warmup' to box-breath's blocks if it is to serve as arrival) so the dose prose and the engine-produced secs agree rather than diverging silently.

Verified against the actual repo, not the truncated 6KB the prior gate saw. fast-feet is a fully authored, panel-vetted entry (verdict approved_with_modification, isNew:true) in you-got-this-design/library/movements.json (lines 2504-2565) with a documented caption-only fallback pose plan (posesNote) and a HARD super_sweaty screening gate + pelvic-floor self-check in its own contraindications. All accessibility substitutions (heel-digs, sit-to-stand, legs-up, marching, step-touch) also exist as vetted moves in movements.json. No [UNKNOWN ... TRUE DEFECT] id is referenced.

Clinical-design judgment (5 axes): cumulativeLoadOK PASS — lean one-couplet structure is correct for a 7-min super_sweaty budget; both breath items <=90s (arrive 30s, wind-down 55s); single quality squat set (12-15 reps, 2s bottom pause) is sane; estTotalSecs 427 matches the entire shipped nt-7-*-pp band (421-427), so it fits the 7-min target band. tierCeilingCompliant PASS — no crunch/sit-up/floor-plank/jump/impact; fast-feet is explicitly low-impact (feet barely leave floor, never hop), squats are closed-chain invisible-chair. screeningGatesCorrect PASS — declares the correct Super Sweaty gate item-by-item (>=12 wks + provider-cleared + symptom-free), routes failures to gentler tiers with the standard disclaimer, and carries an at-any-point stop-rule. accessibilityComplete PASS — declares reduced-motion AND a concrete named chair/floor path. pelvicFloorDiastasisSafe PASS overall — control established fresh before vigor (arrival box-breath screening checkpoint + warm-up that explicitly protects the pelvic floor), low-impact cardio, MANDATORY longer down-regulating wind-down (55s, protected), session-wide doming/leaking/heaviness stop-rule declared, no banned moves. The safety envelope is intact, so pass=true with minor residual cueing fixes below.

Residual gaps are real but minor and confined to the main couplet's in-flow cueing, not the safety envelope.
requiredFixes: Add an explicit dedicated recovery/breath beat between the cardio burst (fast-feet) and the loaded squat hold within the main block, rather than relying only on fast-feet's terminal 20s recovery march; a brief metered breath beat before loading dissipates the peak intra-abdominal-pressure cycling from the cardio before adding the closed-chain hold.

Add an explicit exhale-on-exertion cue to the squats item (e.g. 'exhale as you press up out of the bottom pause'); the squat tierNote currently describes the bottom-pause load but never cues breath on the exertion, which is the core anti-doming mechanic the postpartum envelope requires on every loaded rep.

Add an in-session pelvic-floor checkpoint placed immediately after the cardio burst (mirroring fast-feet's own 'if you feel leaking or heaviness, slow to a march' contraindication cue) so the doming/leaking/heaviness self-check is surfaced at the highest-load moment, not only in the pre-session screening gate and the global at-any-point stop-rule.

Minor estimate-honesty / phase-boundary cleanup (not safety-blocking): recompute estTotalSecs with explicit per-move TRANSITION_SECS rather than carrying the shipped 427, and stop describing the squat's ~20s rest as flowing 'into the close' so phase boundaries stay clean and the down-regulation is never accounted against the close.
